nodes.name: Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: A walled frontier town at the center of the Blackwater Crossing region of Belerion. A stone bridge spans the Blackwater River here. The Old Northern Trade Road passes directly through the town. It is the Heart of the Basilisk Campaign — the starting point. Inside the town: The Laughing Basilisk tavern, The Dragon's Breath Inn, a temple, a market, tunnels beneath the streets, and a cursed well.

nodes.name: The Laughing Basilisk
nodes.description: A tavern inside Blackwater Crossing, near the north gate. It is the party's home base in the Basilisk Campaign.

nodes.name: The Sweaty Mermaid
nodes.description: A tavern on the east (bad) side of the Blackwater River in Blackwater Crossing. It is connected to the suspicious meat rumor. Below the building are stairs leading down to old tunnels beneath the city, including a vampire brothel.

nodes.name: The Twist
nodes.description: A maze of alleys near the Laughing Basilisk in Blackwater Crossing. It is controlled by a local thieves guild known as a "canting crew." A dangerous, labyrinthine district in the heart of town.

nodes.name: Tunnels Beneath Blackwater Crossing
nodes.description: Old tunnels under the city, accessed through stairs beneath a crumbling building near the Sweaty Mermaid on the east side of town. Immediately inside is an arch with a beaded curtain leading to a vampire brothel. The brothel has multiple rooms, a receptionist with a giant red-eyed rat, and at least one vampire. Clients from town visit using pseudonyms. A noble patron buys blood on a schedule before the new moon. Connected to the mystery of the missing children and the fog.

nodes.name: Farms
nodes.description: Farms along the Old Northern Trade Road near Blackwater Crossing. Small buildings, fields, and tilled land supporting the town.

nodes.name: The Abandoned Farmhouse
nodes.description: A ruined farmstead near Blackwater Crossing. It has a basement. There was a fire here.

nodes.name: The Graveyard
nodes.description: A Graveyard is located on the Old Northern Trade Road, about 2 hours south of the Old Forest edge. It serves as a landmark for navigation to the Kalla Cave.

nodes.name: Kalla Cave
nodes.description: The Kalla Cave is located 4 hours east of the Graveyard on the Old Northern Trade Road. It contains a large supply of kalla (food). The party previously discovered it. Whip (PC) convinced the Mud Tooth Porcs (Soak Tusk, Warm Wallow, Steam Snout) to relocate here from the Mud Wallow hot spring to escape the spiders, Pine Troll, and Feather Clan threats.

nodes.name: The Old Forest
nodes.description: A dense, ancient woodland north and northeast of Blackwater Crossing. The Old Northern Trade Road passes through it. Contains dire wolves, giant spiders, kobold hunters, porcs, shadow mastiffs, pine trolls, a vampire runestone, barrow wights, and corrupted groves. Also called the Old Wood.

nodes.name: The Mud Wallow
nodes.description: The Mud Wallow is a hot spring with sulfur-smelling water located west of the Old Northern Trade Road in the Old Forest, north of the Thornespire Keep turnoff. A stream of steaming water flows east from the spring and crosses the road. The hot spring mud is therapeutic — heals 1 Heart per hour of soaking. Home territory of the Mud Tooth tribe of Porcs.

nodes.name: Pjörk Choppe Hille
nodes.description: A porc settlement located in the Old Forest, approximately 3/4 day north of Blackwater Crossing along the Old Northern Trade Road, then west off-road into the woods. Access routes from the Trade Road: widest cart path (direct) ~1 hour, narrower trail ~3 hours, off-path through black pine forest ~6 hours. Several trails lead to the settlement, some wide enough to carry off stolen carts, suggesting organized raiding activity. The porcs here are organized and have been a recurring threat in the region.

nodes.name: The Spider Cave
nodes.description: The Spider Cave at Pjörk Choppe Hille is located right behind the hill, about 1 hour of scrambling over rocks from the settlement. Very close on a map — essentially the same location as Pjörk Choppe Hille. Contains daemon spiders with writings on them, and tunnels below leading to a skeletal lord's throne room, Evil Chaos Shrine, store room, and torture chamber.

nodes.name: Crag Cairn
nodes.description: The Crag Cairn (Barrow Wight) is located southeast of the Mud Wallow hot spring and 2 hours north of the Kobold Crevasse. It contains the Crag King — a barrow wight chieftain sitting on a throne of bones with eight skeleton guards. The dead zone around the mound is growing. A Thornespire Keep helmet is among the throne bones. The party scouted it but did not engage — it's a Level 5 encounter. The Mud Tooth Porcs guided the party there, circumnavigating the spider nests.

nodes.name: The Kobold Crevasse
nodes.description: A location east off the main road in the Old Forest, south of Thornespire Keep. A deep crevasse with a path leading down to a kobold settlement. At the surface is a mystic ring of trees with a stone table bearing thaumaturgical runes — a stone binding spell for witnessing prophetic dreams as the shadow moves across the moon. Home to Yip-Snap, a mighty kobold hunter, and her clan. The kobolds trade and communicate via drums (trash-talking the porcs).

nodes.name: The Fae Glade
nodes.description: A section of old-growth forest between Pjörk Choppe Hille and Thornespire Keep, about 6 hours through the wood from the Pjörk Choppe Hille area. It contains very old trees with silver or white bark that appear to have faces, like a curated garden with paths. There is a significant time differential — minutes spent inside pass as hours in the outside world. The party discovered it while traveling from Pjörk Choppe Hille to Thornespire Keep. Also called "The Garden."

nodes.name: Thornespire Keep
nodes.description: A stone fortress atop a tall hill in the Old Forest, commanding views of the river and surrounding woodland. Reached by traveling north along the Old Northern Trade Road from Blackwater Crossing (about 1 day to the forest edge), then taking a well-maintained road west for about 4 hours (~1.5 days total). The main north-south road is visible from the keep's towers. Contains an Outer Bailey with a Traveler's Inn, a Main Gate, and a Chapel of Muir. The Castellan governs from the Inner Bailey.

nodes.name: Raven's Perch
nodes.description: An abandoned watchtower north of Thornespire Keep. It was the site where the Castellan's missing scouts disappeared. The party investigated and rescued three scouts. The Castellan held a feast to celebrate and granted the party passes to the Inner Bailey.

nodes.name: The Swamp
nodes.description: Northwest of Blackwater Crossing. It has lizardmen and a crazy old hermit who drinks ice wine and knows things. The region was once populated until it was flooded — good place to look for treasure.

nodes.name: Mistwood Glen
nodes.description: A mystical sanctuary deep in the woods, about 2-3 days southwest of Blackwater Crossing through tough forest terrain. The Kalla'din is a pocket realm / Node that exists partially outside normal space and time — an aerial sanctuary woven between three massive oak trees, with platforms 20 feet above scorched ground, floating rune-carved stones, upward-flowing waterfalls, and luminous silver-bark trees. Home to seven immortal Dwur Eldar called the Thornbeard Circle, led by Gunnar "Brambleheart." Snezynja is an Aethiiri sorceress from the stellar courts. The Stone-Nose Clan are ancient forest trolls dwelling at the Standing Stones.

nodes.name: Serpent's Teeth Crags
nodes.description: The Serpent's Teeth Crags is a massive jagged mountain range south of Blackwater Crossing. The crags span a week of travel in both directions on each side of the Old Northern Trade Road — an enormous range. The road passes through a mountain pass here. About 2 days travel south from Blackwater Crossing to the road pass. Contains gargoyles, a brigands' camp, an obsidian quarry, and the Vault of First Light (1 day overland from the road into a pass between crags).

nodes.name: Brunhilde's Mountain Watch Camp
nodes.description: Brunhilde Ironfoot's Mountain Watch Camp & Inn, a waypoint and recruiting camp along the south road in the Serpent's Teeth foothills. Brunhilde runs both the inn and recruits for the Mountain Watch. Has a moat (which contained a gelatinous ooze). Encounters in the area include lightning blights and wyverns. Baron Thornwise (gold wheat sheaf on blue banner) claims jurisdiction via a tax collector agent. Dwur patrols operate in the nearby mountain foothills.

nodes.name: The Bandit Camp
nodes.description: The Bandit Camp at Hangman Hill is located directly north of the Tower of the Stargazer, about 1 hour walk, just inside the edge of the Old Forest. A large operation: 120 people total — 48 bandits and 72 slaves. Key NPCs: Krin "The Cunning" (strike force leader, natural tactician, commands 20-person raiding force), Rath "Iron-Will" (slaver boss/quarry overseer, carries dubious legal writ for "contract labor"), Corth "Whip-Crack" (the pit whip, doesn't speak). The obsidian quarry is a crater gouged into shale with a single large obsidian spire at its center that may be hollow or deeper than it appears — digging at the spire base is forbidden on pain of death. Bandit Hill is a separate ambush site overlooking the main road, connected to Hangman Hill's water grotto via underground streambed. Bandits marked trees with carved serpent symbols, suggesting plans beyond lumber cutting. The camp was ultimately destroyed by lightning from the Tower of the Stargazer.

nodes.name: Tower of the Stargazer
nodes.description: Home of Sir Uravulon Calcidius, an evil wizard corrupted by contact with beings from other worlds. Located about 2 days south of Blackwater Crossing along the Old Northern Trade Road, then half a day west, in the foothills north of the Serpent's Teeth Crags. The tower is 80 feet tall, solid stone blocks, no visible windows. Top section resembles a globe with layered metal plate dome. Ring of steel spikes around the rim, and four 40-foot steel spikes jut from the ground 50 feet from the base, all leaning inward. The area between the spikes is a blasted lunar-like wasteland. Lightning strikes constantly, even in cloudless weather.

nodes.name: Vault of First Light
nodes.description: The Vault of First Light is an ancient dwarven dungeon in the Serpent's Teeth. Reached by traveling 2 days south from Blackwater Crossing along the Old Northern Trade Road, then 1 day overland through hills and up into a pass between crags. The party has explored it and returned with a magical candle that speaks in ancient tongues. Contains rooms, traps, the Fallen (undead dwur), and connections to cosmic forces.

links.name: Forest Track to Pjörk Choppe Hille
